title: Fix release distribution Git isolation

sources.reason: The public distribution fixture currently exports repository binding variables into nested installer commands.
Contains the Git isolation regression coverage for the public distribution journey.
Shows adoption scope validation resolving the caller repository commit instead of the synthetic project commit.

riskAssessment.reason: The checker exercises a public installer through nested Git and Make processes, so ambient repository bindings must not leak.

executionDecision.reason: The failing baseCommit and current environment propagation identify a bounded checker defect.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Public distribution fixture commands resolve Git state from the synthetic project repository.
Nested installer and Make processes receive no GIT_DIR or GIT_WORK_TREE binding.
Hostile ambient Git variables remain isolated without mutating the caller environment.
Focused release distribution tests and full project quality checks pass.
guidelines: Do not mutate the process-wide environment while isolating release checks.
Use repository cwd and GIT_CEILING_DIRECTORIES instead of propagating repository binding variables.
Keep release validation fail-closed when the synthetic repository cannot be proven.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
